seo.title: Building Code Generation with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with Vercel AI SDK enables powerful streaming UIs for iterative code creation, but introduces high risks like malicious code injection and execution failures. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code safety, syntax, and sandbox execution before deployment. This guide shows how to integrate Vex middleware into your Vercel AI SDK workflows for reliable code generation.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with a Vercel AI SDK streamText call that generates code via an LLM agent, often using structured tools for sandbox execution and testing. The generated code is streamed to React Server Components for real-time UI updates, with iterative refinement based on test results. Vex middleware intercepts generateText/streamText calls to validate outputs against safety and syntax rules before they reach the UI or executor.

A typical flow involves: 1) User prompt → AI SDK agent → Vex verification → Sandbox execution → Test validation → Refined code output. This ensures consistency across providers like OpenAI or Anthropic while mitigating edge timeouts on complex tasks.

Edge functions handle streaming to minimize latency, but require Vex to prevent partial hallucinations from propagating to users.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Incomplete code snippets stream to UI before full generation, causing syntax errors or unsafe partial code execution
risks.mitigation: Vex buffers and validates complete structured outputs before streaming

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Unverified sandbox execution results shown to users, potentially exposing malicious behavior
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs match expected schemas and safety constraints

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs produce varying code formats, breaking downstream parsers and executors
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes structured outputs to consistent JSON schemas

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Long-running code validation and iteration exceed Vercel edge limits
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification with configurable timeouts and partial result handling
